subject: Re:
body:
i will be in LA on 10th evening ,vegas 17 th london on dubai on 18th and london on 20th&21st and 22nd till 26th cape town , zimbabwe ,mozambique, 26th rawanda and 27th london 29th dubai










-----Original Message-----

From: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>

To: ssulayem@aol.com

Sent: Mon, 9 Jun 2008 7:00 pm

Subject: Re:





where will you be next week, I'll probably be in paris.    ----- Original Message ----  From: "ssulayem@aol.com" <ssulayem@aol.com>  To: Jeffrey Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>  Sent: Monday, June 2, 2008 7:58:02 AM  Subject: Re:    Dear Jeffrey    There is no clear direction or guidance on a de pegging of the Dirham. Recent   articles point to the fact that a de peg alone will not resolve the UAE's or the   GCC's inflationary issues, with Kuwait being a case in hand. If the intent is to   make a play, then one option, subject to trading levels, would be to buy back   part or all of Dubai World Jafza's AED 7.5 billion Sukuk(bond).  Separately, Abu   Dhabi is also understood to be looking at an AED issue. Alternatively and likely   a quicker entry point would be to enter into a series of private placements with   a number of key parties.    Investment Office Of Dubai would potentially be interested, at the right price   and size  ------Original Message------  From: Jeffrey Epstein  To: Ssulayem  Sent: 2 Jun 2008 4:29 AM  Subject:     sultan ,, i think a great investment would be local currency denominated debt in   abu dahbi, and qatar,  the peg to the dollar probably will not last. where can   we buy a big bunch      Sent from my BlackBerry® wireless device
